content:
**Solution.**  Write 
\[
\ell(a,b)=\mathrm{lcm}(a,b)=\frac{a\,b}{\gcd(a,b)},\qquad
\ell(a,391)=\frac{391a}{\gcd(a,391)}.
\]
We want 
\[
\ell(a,b)>\ell(a,391)
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\frac{ab}{\gcd(a,b)}>\frac{391a}{\gcd(a,391)}
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
b\;\gcd(a,391)\;>\;391\;\gcd(a,b).
\]
Since \(a<391\), \(\gcd(a,391)\) can only be \(1,17\) or \(23\).  To make the left‐hand side large and the right small one takes
\[
\gcd(a,391)=23,
\quad
\gcd(a,b)=1,
\]
and then the inequality reads
\[
23\,b>391\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad b>\frac{391}{23}=17.
\]
Hence the smallest integer \(b\) that works is \(b=18\), for instance with \(a=23\).  Indeed
\[
\ell(23,18)=\frac{23\cdot18}{1}=414,
\quad
\ell(23,391)=391,
\]
and \(414>391\).  No smaller \(b\) can satisfy the inequality.  Therefore the minimum value of \(b\) among all “pupusa’’ pairs is
\[
\boxed{18}.
\]

\[
\ell(a,b)=\mathrm{lcm}(a,b)=\frac{a\,b}{\gcd(a,b)},\qquad
\ell(a,391)=\frac{391a}{\gcd(a,391)}.

\[
\ell(a,b)>\ell(a,391)

\frac{ab}{\gcd(a,b)}>\frac{391a}{\gcd(a,391)}
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
b\;\gcd(a,391)\;>\;391\;\gcd(a,b).
\]
Since \(a<391\), \(\gcd(a,391)\) can only be \(1,17\) or \(23\).  To make the left‐hand side large and the right small one takes
\[
\gcd(a,391)=23,
\quad
\gcd(a,b)=1,
\]

\[

\[
\boxed{18}.
